exercises.title: Cache stampede — count the DB queries
exercises.expected_solution:
1. 'DB QUERY: P001' appears UP TO 1000 times (once per concurrent thread)
   In practice: limited by HikariCP pool=10 → first 10 succeed,
   remaining 990 queue waiting for a DB connection.

2. The other 990 requests:
   - Wait in HikariCP connection queue
   - If connection-timeout=2000ms: after 2 seconds → HikariPool exception
   - Result: HTTP 503 errors for most users
   - DB under maximum stress for 2-3 seconds
   - Even with pool=10, 10 queries for IDENTICAL data is wasteful!

3. SETNX fix:
   Before querying DB, call SETNX('products::P001::lock', '1', TTL=10s)
   Only ONE thread gets 'true' → that thread queries DB, fills cache
   Other 999 threads get 'false' → wait 50ms → retry cache read
   → 1 DB query instead of up to 1000

test_cases.expected:
1. 'DB QUERY: P001' appears UP TO 1000 times (once per concurrent thread)
   In practice: limited by HikariCP pool=10 → first 10 succeed,
   remaining 990 queue waiting for a DB connection.

2. The other 990 requests:
   - Wait in HikariCP connection queue
   - If connection-timeout=2000ms: after 2 seconds → HikariPool exception
   - Result: HTTP 503 errors for most users
   - DB under maximum stress for 2-3 seconds
   - Even with pool=10, 10 queries for IDENTICAL data is wasteful!

3. SETNX fix:
   Before querying DB, call SETNX('products::P001::lock', '1', TTL=10s)
   Only ONE thread gets 'true' → that thread queries DB, fills cache
   Other 999 threads get 'false' → wait 50ms → retry cache read
   → 1 DB query instead of up to 1000

hints: @Cacheable has no stampede protection — concurrent misses all hit DB
HikariCP pool limits concurrent DB connections but requests still queue
SETNX (set if not exists) is atomic — only one thread wins the lock

exercises.title: Complete the cache-aside pattern
exercises.scenario: Manual cache-aside: check → miss → DB → store → return.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to complete the cache-aside implementation.
hints: opsForValue().get(key): Redis GET — returns null if key doesn't exist
opsForValue().set(key, value, duration): Redis SET key value EX seconds
ifPresent: only store in cache if result is present (don't cache null)

exercises.title: Write a manual cache-aside service
exercises.scenario: Implement get-or-load pattern with explicit cache storage.
exercises.instructions:
Write CategoryCacheService.findById(Long id):
1. Check Redis for 'category:{id}'
2. On miss: query DB, store in Redis with 1-hour TTL
3. On hit: return from Redis
4. delete(Long id): removes from DB and Redis
5. update(Category c): saves to DB and updates Redis
hints: PREFIX + id builds the Redis key: 'category:5'
delete: remove from DB first, then evict from Redis (order matters)
update: always set with full TTL to reset the expiry timer

exercises.title: Redis Hash — user profile storage
exercises.scenario: Hash stores structured data with field-level access.
exercises.instructions:
Write UserProfileHashService:
1. cacheProfile(UserProfile): store fields in Redis Hash with 2-hour TTL
2. getProfile(userId): read full profile from Hash
3. updateCity(userId, newCity): update ONLY the city field
4. getPlan(userId): read ONLY the plan field
5. Show the Redis commands each method generates
hints: opsForHash().putAll(key, map): HSET with multiple fields at once
opsForHash().put(key, field, value): HSET for a single field
opsForHash().get(key, field): HGET — gets one field without loading all

exercises.title: Redis Sorted Set — real-time leaderboard
exercises.scenario: Sorted Set provides O(log N) rank operations — perfect for leaderboards.
exercises.instructions:
Write a TradingLeaderboard:
1. recordPnL(traderId, amount): add/increment score (ZINCRBY)
2. getRank(traderId): rank from top (0 = first place) using ZREVRANK
3. getTopN(n): return top N trader IDs
4. getTopNWithScores(n): return List of Map with traderId + profit
5. resetDailyBoard(): delete the key at end of day
hints: reverseRank: rank 0 = highest score; rank() would give 0 = lowest score
reverseRange(key, 0, n-1): indices are inclusive, 0-based
reverseRangeWithScores: returns TypedTuple with value + score

exercises.title: Null-object caching pattern
exercises.scenario: Cache 'not found' results to prevent repeated DB queries for missing keys.
exercises.instructions:
Write InstrumentCacheService with null-object pattern:
1. findBySymbol(symbol): cache hit → return; null sentinel → empty; miss → DB
2. Cache real instruments for 6 hours
3. Cache null sentinel '__NONE__' for 5 minutes
4. Store and retrieve: show the sentinel logic clearly
5. Test: same invalid symbol returns empty without hitting DB twice
hints: Sentinel TTL must be SHORTER than real data TTL — the entity may be created soon
NONE.equals(cached): null-safe check (cached could be null on actual miss)
instanceof pattern match: if (cached instanceof Instrument i) avoids cast

exercises.title: Cache stampede prevention with mutex lock
exercises.scenario: SETNX acquires a distributed lock so only one thread rebuilds the cache.
exercises.instructions:
Write StampedeSafeProductService.findById(id):
1. Fast path: check cache, return if found
2. Acquire lock: setIfAbsent('product:{id}:lock', '1', TTL=10s)
3. If lock acquired: double-check cache, query DB, store, release lock
4. If lock NOT acquired: sleep 50ms, retry cache read, fallback to DB if still empty
5. Show why double-check is necessary
hints: setIfAbsent with TTL: atomic SETNX + EXPIRE in one operation
try-finally for lock release: ensures lock is released even on exception
Double-check: re-read cache after acquiring lock before querying DB

exercises.title: Multi-level cache: Caffeine L1 + Redis L2
exercises.scenario: Ultra-hot data benefits from an in-JVM L1 cache (nanoseconds) in front of Redis.
exercises.instructions:
Configure a two-level cache:
1. Caffeine L1 cache (in-JVM, 200 entries, 30-second TTL)
2. Redis L2 cache (distributed, 10-minute TTL)
3. ProductCacheService that checks L1 first, then L2, then DB
4. Write through: update L2, invalidate L1
5. Show latency comparison: L1=0.01ms, L2=0.3ms, DB=15ms
hints: L1 invalidate on write: must invalidate L1 on every instance (or use short TTL)
Caffeine.newBuilder().maximumSize(N): evicts via LRU when N is exceeded
Backfill L1 from L2 hit: next request served from L1 (faster)

exercises.title: Leaderboard with daily reset and rank retrieval
exercises.scenario: Full Zerodha-style trading leaderboard with persistence at day end.
exercises.instructions:
Build DailyPnLLeaderboard:
1. recordTrade(traderId, pnl): ZINCRBY
2. getTopTradersWithScores(n): returns List<TraderRank> with position, id, pnl
3. getMyRank(traderId): rank + score for a specific trader
4. endOfDay(): snapshot top 10 to PostgreSQL, then DEL the Redis key
5. @DataJpaTest-level logic test: verify rank order after 3 trades
hints: reverseRank is 0-indexed; add 1 for human-readable position
TypedTuple: getValue() = member, getScore() = score (null-safe check)
endOfDay: snapshot before delete — data lost after DEL!

exercises.title: What Redis commands does this code generate?
exercises.scenario: Understand the Redis wire protocol behind Spring's RedisTemplate calls.
exercises.expected_solution:
redisTemplate.opsForValue().set(..., Duration.ofMinutes(10))
  → SET product:P001 '{...json...}' EX 600

redisTemplate.opsForValue().get('product:P001')
  → GET product:P001

redisTemplate.expire('product:P001', Duration.ofMinutes(5))
  → EXPIRE product:P001 300

redisTemplate.opsForZSet().incrementScore('board', 'Aisha', 500)
  → ZINCRBY board 500 Aisha

redisTemplate.opsForZSet().reverseRank('board', 'Aisha')
  → ZREVRANK board Aisha

redisTemplate.opsForZSet().reverseRange('board', 0, 2)
  → ZREVRANGE board 0 2

redisTemplate.opsForHash().put('user:U001', 'city', 'Mumbai')
  → HSET user:U001 city Mumbai

redisTemplate.opsForValue().setIfAbsent('lock:P001', '1', Duration.ofSeconds(10))
  → SET lock:P001 1 NX EX 10
  (SET with NX=only if not exists, EX=expiry seconds)

redisTemplate.delete('lock:P001')
  → DEL lock:P001

test_cases.expected:
redisTemplate.opsForValue().set(..., Duration.ofMinutes(10))
  → SET product:P001 '{...json...}' EX 600

redisTemplate.opsForValue().get('product:P001')
  → GET product:P001

redisTemplate.expire('product:P001', Duration.ofMinutes(5))
  → EXPIRE product:P001 300

redisTemplate.opsForZSet().incrementScore('board', 'Aisha', 500)
  → ZINCRBY board 500 Aisha

redisTemplate.opsForZSet().reverseRank('board', 'Aisha')
  → ZREVRANK board Aisha

redisTemplate.opsForZSet().reverseRange('board', 0, 2)
  → ZREVRANGE board 0 2

redisTemplate.opsForHash().put('user:U001', 'city', 'Mumbai')
  → HSET user:U001 city Mumbai

redisTemplate.opsForValue().setIfAbsent('lock:P001', '1', Duration.ofSeconds(10))
  → SET lock:P001 1 NX EX 10
  (SET with NX=only if not exists, EX=expiry seconds)

redisTemplate.delete('lock:P001')
  → DEL lock:P001

hints: set() with Duration: SET key value EX seconds
setIfAbsent with TTL: SET key value NX EX seconds (atomic SETNX+EXPIRE)
ZINCRBY: increment score (not set); ZADD is for setting absolute score

exercises.title: Background cache refresh — prevent miss latency
exercises.scenario: @Scheduled refreshes cache before TTL expires so users never see a miss.
exercises.instructions:
Write a BackgroundCacheRefreshService:
1. Track which keys are actively used (record access via INCR on 'hot:keys')
2. @Scheduled every 8 minutes: refresh keys that had > 10 accesses
3. Refresh: load from DB and update Redis (re-set with full TTL)
4. Benefit: users never see a cache miss for hot data
5. Show the Redis TTL check before deciding to refresh
hints: redis.getExpire(key): returns TTL in seconds (-1 = no expiry, -2 = key doesn't exist)
Refresh threshold: when TTL < N minutes remaining, refresh now
Reset counters after each cycle: prevents unbounded Hash growth

exercises.title: Flipkart deal cache — full cache-aside with stampede protection
exercises.scenario: Production cache-aside with stampede prevention and null-object pattern.
exercises.instructions:
Build Flipkart's DealCacheService:
1. getTopDeals(city, date): cache-aside with 5-minute TTL
2. Stampede protection via mutex lock (10-second lock TTL)
3. Null sentinel for cities with no deals (1-minute TTL)
4. refreshDeals(city, date): programmatic cache invalidation + rebuild
5. 2 tests: verify stampede returns results, verify null sentinel avoids DB hit
hints: Null sentinel + stampede: both patterns can be combined in one method
try-finally: ALWAYS release lock, even if DB query throws
refreshDeals: delete + immediately rebuild = cache pre-warming after invalidation

exercises.title: Redis List as activity feed (recent N events)
exercises.scenario: Redis List stores ordered sequences — perfect for activity feeds.
exercises.instructions:
Write UserActivityFeedService:
1. pushActivity(userId, activity): LPUSH + LTRIM to keep last 20 events
2. getRecentActivities(userId, n): LRANGE to get n most recent
3. clearFeed(userId): DEL the key
4. Show: LPUSH + LTRIM is an O(1) fixed-size queue
5. Integration test verifying max 20 activities retained
hints: LPUSH: adds to head (left) of list — newest entry is always at index 0
LTRIM 0 (MAX-1): keeps only first MAX elements, removes the rest
LRANGE 0 (n-1): gets first n elements from head — most recent activities

exercises.title: Swiggy search cache — complete cache-aside with stampede protection
exercises.scenario: Production search caching with all patterns combined.
exercises.instructions:
Build Swiggy's RestaurantSearchCache:
1. search(city, cuisine, maxDelivery): composite key + cache-aside + stampede
2. Null sentinel for no-results searches (2-minute TTL)
3. Results cached for 3 minutes
4. Mutex lock with 5-second TTL
5. @SpringBootTest-style test (compile-only): verify method signatures

All code compiles.
hints: Composite key lowercase: prevents 'Mumbai' and 'mumbai' from being different keys
try-finally: critical — lock MUST be released even if DB throws
Both null sentinel and real results protected by same mutex

exercises.title: Complete Zerodha trading cache synthesis — all patterns
exercises.scenario: Multi-pattern Redis service: leaderboard + cache-aside + null-object + stampede.
exercises.instructions:
Build complete ZerodhaCacheHub:
1. TraderLeaderboard (Sorted Set): recordTrade, getTop10, getMyRank
2. InstrumentCache (cache-aside + null sentinel): findBySymbol
3. MarketStatus (String + stampede): getMarketStatus() — high contention on market open
4. UserSessionCache (Hash): saveSession, getSessionField, invalidate
5. Test (compile + run): record 3 trades, verify leaderboard order

All code compiles and test runs.
exercises.starter_code: // Complete Zerodha cache hub — all Redis patterns in one service
hints: Two RedisTemplates: String template for leaderboard/session, Object for complex types
reverseRank + 1: convert 0-indexed to 1-indexed for display
All patterns in one service: real Zerodha service would split into separate components
